req.title: Device data ingest (negative)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for FHIR Ingest – Device Registration & Observation Push
Requirement:
  - A registered device SHALL push Observations via FHIR ingest.
  - Unregistered devices SHALL be rejected.
  - Negative paths: permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
    conflicting state transitions.
"""

import copy
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-under-test imports (adjust paths to match your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from fhir_ingest.client import FHIRIngestClient          # thin HTTP wrapper
from fhir_ingest.device_registry import DeviceRegistry   # manages device state
from fhir_ingest.exceptions import (
    DeviceNotRegisteredError,
    DuplicateDeviceError,
    FHIRValidationError,
    IngestPermissionError,
    InvalidStateTransitionError,
    MissingMandatoryElementError,
)
from fhir_ingest.models import Device, Observation        # Pydantic / dataclass models
from fhir_ingest.service import FHIRIngestService         # orchestrates everything

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-001"
UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-999"
VALID_PATIENT_ID = "patient-abc"
BASE_URL = "https://fhir.example.com"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def registered_device() -> Device:
    return Device(
        id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
        identifier="SN-REG-001",
        status="active",
        owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
        permissions=["observation:write"],
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def unregistered_device() -> Device:
    return Device(
        id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
        identifier="SN-UNREG-999",
        status=None,          # never persisted to registry
        owner_patient_id=None,
        permissions=[],
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_device_registry(registered_device) -> MagicMock:
    registry = MagicMock(spec=DeviceRegistry)
    registry.is_registered.side_effect = (
        lambda device_id: device_id == REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
    )
    registry.get_device.side_effect = lambda device_id: (
        registered_device if device_id == REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
        else (_ for _ in ()).throw(DeviceNotRegisteredError(device_id))
    )
    return registry


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_fhir_client() -> MagicMock:
    client = MagicMock(spec=FHIRIngestClient)
    client.base_url = BASE_URL
    # Default happy-path response
    client.post_observation.return_value = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
    }
    return client


@pytest.fixture()
def ingest_service(mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client) -> FHIRIngestService:
    return FHIRIngestService(
        device_registry=mock_device_registry,
        fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_observation() -> dict:
    """Minimal valid FHIR Observation resource."""
    return {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
        "code": {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": "http://loinc.org",
                    "code": "8867-4",
                    "display": "Heart rate",
                }
            ]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{VALID_PATIENT_ID}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "device": {"reference": f"Device/{REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID}"},
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": 72,
            "unit": "beats/min",
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": "/min",
        },
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _drop_key(d: dict, *keys: str) -> dict:
    """Return a deep copy of d with the given dot-path keys removed."""
    result = copy.deepcopy(d)
    for key_path in keys:
        parts = key_path.split(".")
        node = result
        for part in parts[:-1]:
            node = node[part]
        del node[parts[-1]]
    return result


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY PATH – registered device can push Observations
# ===========================================================================


class TestRegisteredDevicePushObservation:
    def test_push_observation_returns_created_resource(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        """Registered device successfully pushes a valid Observation."""
        result = ingest_service.ingest_observation(
            device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
            observation=valid_observation,
        )
        assert result["resourceType"] == "Observation"
        assert result["status"] == "final"

    def test_push_observation_calls_fhir_client_once(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        ingest_service.ingest_observation(
            device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
            observation=valid_observation,
        )
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.assert_called_once()

    def test_push_observation_payload_contains_device_reference(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        ingest_service.ingest_observation(
            device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
            observation=valid_observation,
        )
        posted_payload = mock_fhir_client.post_observation.call_args[0][0]
        assert f"Device/{REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID}" in str(posted_payload)

    def test_push_multiple_observations_sequentially(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        for _ in range(5):
            obs = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
            obs["id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
            obs["valueQuantity"]["value"] = 70 + _
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=obs,
            )
        assert mock_fhir_client.post_observation.call_count == 5

    def test_push_observation_with_component_array(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        """Observation with optional component array is accepted."""
        valid_observation["component"] = [
            {
                "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8480-6"}]},
                "valueQuantity": {"value": 120, "unit": "mmHg"},
            }
        ]
        result = ingest_service.ingest_observation(
            device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
            observation=valid_observation,
        )
        assert result is not None


# ===========================================================================
# 2. UNREGISTERED DEVICE – must be rejected
# ===========================================================================


class TestUnregisteredDeviceRejection:
    def test_unregistered_device_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = f"Device/{UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID}"
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_fhir_client_not_called_for_unregistered_device(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = f"Device/{UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID}"
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.assert_not_called()

    def test_unregistered_device_error_contains_device_id(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = f"Device/{UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID}"
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError) as exc_info:
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )
        assert UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID in str(exc_info.value)

    def test_empty_string_device_id_rejected(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        with pytest.raises((DeviceNotRegisteredError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_none_device_id_rejected(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        with pytest.raises((DeviceNotRegisteredError, TypeError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=None,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_sql_injection_style_device_id_rejected(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        with pytest.raises((DeviceNotRegisteredError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="' OR 1=1 --",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 3. PERMISSION DENIAL
# ===========================================================================


class TestPermissionDenial:
    def test_device_without_observation_write_permission_is_denied(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        restricted_device = Device(
            id="device-restricted",
            identifier="SN-RESTRICTED",
            status="active",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=[],               # no observation:write
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = restricted_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-restricted"

        with pytest.raises(IngestPermissionError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-restricted",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_read_only_permission_does_not_allow_ingest(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        read_only_device = Device(
            id="device-ro",
            identifier="SN-RO",
            status="active",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:read"],
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = read_only_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-ro"

        with pytest.raises(IngestPermissionError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-ro",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_inactive_device_is_denied(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        inactive_device = Device(
            id="device-inactive",
            identifier="SN-INACTIVE",
            status="inactive",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:write"],
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = inactive_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-inactive"

        with pytest.raises(IngestPermissionError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-inactive",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_suspended_device_is_denied(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        suspended_device = Device(
            id="device-suspended",
            identifier="SN-SUSP",
            status="suspended",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:write"],
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = suspended_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-suspended"

        with pytest.raises(IngestPermissionError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-suspended",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_permission_error_does_not_call_fhir_client(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        no_perm_device = Device(
            id="device-noperm",
            identifier="SN-NP",
            status="active",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=[],
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = no_perm_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-noperm"

        with pytest.raises(IngestPermissionError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-noperm",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. MISSING MANDATORY ELEMENTS
# ===========================================================================


class TestMissingMandatoryElements:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "missing_field",
        [
            "resourceType",
            "status",
            "code",
            "subject",
            "effectiveDateTime",
            "device",
        ],
    )
    def test_missing_top_level_mandatory_field_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation, missing_field
    ):
        incomplete = _drop_key(valid_observation, missing_field)
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=incomplete,
            )

    def test_missing_code_coding_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        incomplete = _drop_key(valid_observation, "code.coding")
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=incomplete,
            )

    def test_empty_code_coding_array_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        invalid = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
        invalid["code"]["coding"] = []
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=invalid,
            )

    def test_missing_subject_reference_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        incomplete = _drop_key(valid_observation, "subject.reference")
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=incomplete,
            )

    def test_missing_device_reference_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        incomplete = _drop_key(valid_observation, "device.reference")
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=incomplete,
            )

    def test_empty_observation_dict_raises_error(self, ingest_service):
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation={},
            )

    def test_none_observation_raises_error(self, ingest_service):
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, TypeError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=None,
            )

    def test_wrong_resource_type_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        invalid = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
        invalid["resourceType"] = "Patient"
        with pytest.raises((FHIRValidationError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=invalid,
            )

    def test_invalid_status_value_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        invalid = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
        invalid["status"] = "banana"        # not a valid FHIR Observation status
        with pytest.raises((FHIRValidationError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=invalid,
            )

    def test_malformed_effective_datetime_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        invalid = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
        invalid["effectiveDateTime"] = "not-a-date"
        with pytest.raises((FHIRValidationError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=invalid,
            )

    def test_negative_quantity_value_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        """Heart rate cannot be negative."""
        invalid = copy.deepcopy(valid_observation)
        invalid["valueQuantity"]["value"] = -1
        with pytest.raises((FHIRValidationError, ValueError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=invalid,
            )

    def test_missing_value_and_data_absent_reason_raises_error(
        self, ingest_service, valid_observation
    ):
        """FHIR R4: either value[x] or dataAbsentReason must be present."""
        incomplete = _drop_key(valid_observation, "valueQuantity")
        with pytest.raises((MissingMandatoryElementError, FHIRValidationError)):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=incomplete,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. CONFLICTING STATE TRANSITIONS
# ===========================================================================


class TestConflictingStateTransitions:
    def test_register_already_registered_device_raises_error(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        mock_device_registry.register_device.side_effect = DuplicateDeviceError(
            REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
        )
        with pytest.raises(DuplicateDeviceError):
            mock_device_registry.register_device(
                Device(
                    id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                    identifier="SN-REG-001",
                    status="active",
                    owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
                    permissions=["observation:write"],
                )
            )

    def test_deregister_unregistered_device_raises_error(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        mock_device_registry.deregister_device.side_effect = DeviceNotRegisteredError(
            UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
        )
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError):
            mock_device_registry.deregister_device(UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)

    def test_push_observation_after_deregistration_is_rejected(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        """Simulate deregistration then ingest attempt."""
        # After deregistration the registry no longer recognises the device
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = False
        mock_device_registry.get_device.side_effect = DeviceNotRegisteredError(
            REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
        )

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_transition_active_to_active_is_no_op_or_raises(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        """active → active is a redundant / conflicting transition."""
        mock_device_registry.update_status.side_effect = InvalidStateTransitionError(
            "active", "active"
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            mock_device_registry.update_status(REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID, "active")

    def test_transition_inactive_to_suspended_is_invalid(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        mock_device_registry.update_status.side_effect = InvalidStateTransitionError(
            "inactive", "suspended"
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            mock_device_registry.update_status(REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID, "suspended")

    def test_transition_deregistered_to_suspended_is_invalid(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        mock_device_registry.update_status.side_effect = InvalidStateTransitionError(
            "deregistered", "suspended"
        )
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            mock_device_registry.update_status(REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID, "suspended")

    def test_observation_pushed_during_registration_lock_is_rejected(
        self, mock_device_registry, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        """
        If the registry is in the middle of a registration transaction
        (e.g. status='pending'), observation ingestion must be denied.
        """
        pending_device = Device(
            id="device-pending",
            identifier="SN-PEND",
            status="pending",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:write"],
        )
        mock_device_registry.is_registered.return_value = True
        mock_device_registry.get_device.return_value = pending_device

        service = FHIRIngestService(
            device_registry=mock_device_registry,
            fhir_client=mock_fhir_client,
        )
        valid_observation["device"]["reference"] = "Device/device-pending"

        with pytest.raises((IngestPermissionError, InvalidStateTransitionError)):
            service.ingest_observation(
                device_id="device-pending",
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_concurrent_registration_and_deregistration_raises_error(
        self, mock_device_registry
    ):
        """
        Simulates a race condition where register and deregister are called
        for the same device ID simultaneously.
        """
        call_count = {"n": 0}

        def flaky_register(device):
            call_count["n"] += 1
            if call_count["n"] == 2:
                raise InvalidStateTransitionError("registering", "deregistering")

        mock_device_registry.register_device.side_effect = flaky_register

        device = Device(
            id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
            identifier="SN-REG-001",
            status="active",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:write"],
        )

        # First call succeeds
        mock_device_registry.register_device(device)
        # Second call (simulating race) raises
        with pytest.raises(InvalidStateTransitionError):
            mock_device_registry.register_device(device)


# ===========================================================================
# 6. FHIR CLIENT / NETWORK LAYER ERRORS
# ===========================================================================


class TestFHIRClientErrors:
    def test_fhir_client_500_propagates_exception(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.side_effect = Exception(
            "Internal Server Error"
        )
        with pytest.raises(Exception, match="Internal Server Error"):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_fhir_client_422_unprocessable_entity_raises_validation_error(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.side_effect = FHIRValidationError(
            "Unprocessable Entity"
        )
        with pytest.raises(FHIRValidationError):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )

    def test_fhir_client_timeout_propagates(
        self, ingest_service, mock_fhir_client, valid_observation
    ):
        import socket
        mock_fhir_client.post_observation.side_effect = socket.timeout(
            "Connection timed out"
        )
        with pytest.raises(socket.timeout):
            ingest_service.ingest_observation(
                device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
                observation=valid_observation,
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 7. DEVICE REGISTRATION WORKFLOW (unit-level)
# ===========================================================================


class TestDeviceRegistrationWorkflow:
    def test_register_new_device_succeeds(self, mock_device_registry):
        new_device = Device(
            id="device-new",
            identifier="SN-NEW-002",
            status="active",
            owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
            permissions=["observation:write"],
        )
        mock_device_registry.register_device.return_value = new_device
        result = mock_device_registry.register_device(new_device)
        assert result.id == "device-new"

    def test_registered_device_is_recognised(self, mock_device_registry):
        assert mock_device_registry.is_registered(REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID) is True

    def test_unregistered_device_is_not_recognised(self, mock_device_registry):
        assert mock_device_registry.is_registered(UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID) is False

    def test_get_registered_device_returns_device(
        self, mock_device_registry, registered_device
    ):
        device = mock_device_registry.get_device(REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)
        assert device.id == REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID

    def test_get_unregistered_device_raises_error(self, mock_device_registry):
        with pytest.raises(DeviceNotRegisteredError):
            mock_device_registry.get_device(UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)
```

---

## Design Notes

| Layer | What is tested | Key assertion strategy |
|---|---|---|
| **Happy path** | Registered device pushes valid Observation | Response shape + `post_observation` called once |
| **Unregistered device** | 6 cases – bad ID, empty, `None`, injection strings | `DeviceNotRegisteredError` raised; FHIR client **never** called |
| **Permission denial** | No permissions, read-only, `inactive`, `suspended`, `pending` | `IngestPermissionError`; FHIR client **never** called |
| **Missing mandatory elements** | 12 cases – each top-level field, nested fields, empty arrays, bad status/date, negative value | `MissingMandatoryElementError` or `FHIRValidationError` |
| **State transitions** | re-register, deregister-then-ingest, active→active, race condition | `InvalidStateTransitionError` / `DuplicateDeviceError` |
| **Network layer** | 500, 422, timeout | Exceptions propagate correctly |
| **Registration workflow** | CRUD on registry | Mock boundary verification |

> **Adapting to your codebase:** replace the import paths at the top with your actual module locations. The test logic and assertions are implementation-agnostic; only the exception class names and model constructors need aligning.

Pytest test suite for FHIR Ingest – Device Registration & Observation Push
Requirement:
  - A registered device SHALL push Observations via FHIR ingest.
  - Unregistered devices SHALL be rejected.
  - Negative paths: permission denial, missing mandatory elements,

"""

import copy
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-under-test imports (adjust paths to match your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

from fhir_ingest.device_registry import DeviceRegistry   # manages device state
from fhir_ingest.exceptions import (
    DeviceNotRegisteredError,
    DuplicateDeviceError,
    FHIRValidationError,
    IngestPermissionError,
    InvalidStateTransitionError,
    MissingMandatoryElementError,
)
from fhir_ingest.models import Device, Observation        # Pydantic / dataclass models

VALID_PATIENT_ID = "patient-abc"

# Fixtures

    return Device(
        id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
        identifier="SN-REG-001",
        status="active",
        owner_patient_id=VALID_PATIENT_ID,
        permissions=["observation:write"],
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def unregistered_device() -> Device:
    return Device(
        id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
        identifier="SN-UNREG-999",
        status=None,          # never persisted to registry
        owner_patient_id=None,
        permissions=[],
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_device_registry(registered_device) -> MagicMock:
    registry = MagicMock(spec=DeviceRegistry)
    registry.is_registered.side_effect = (
        lambda device_id: device_id == REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
    )
    registry.get_device.side_effect = lambda device_id: (
        registered_device if device_id == REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID
        else (_ for _ in ()).throw(DeviceNotRegisteredError(device_id))
    )
    return registry


@pytest.fixture()

    client = MagicMock(spec=FHIRIngestClient)
    client.base_url = BASE_URL
    # Default happy-path response
    client.post_observation.return_value = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": "final",
    }
    return client

    )
